_source_of_truth_note: Canonical scenario spec for Wasteland Crystals. Per the operative narrative frame (proposals/PROPOSAL-WASTELAND-CRYSTALS.md §'Update 2026-05-07: Operative narrative frame — mining operation'): the player is a future landfill miner working a cell, not an explorer or curator. Each scenario IS a real ELFM (Enhanced Landfill Mining) site characterization, with phase identifications drawn from peer-reviewed excavation papers wherever possible. The expected_species map links scenarios to mineral catalog entries via evidence_role, distinguishing what the cited paper actually observed from what the chemistry implies but the paper didn't name.

_schema.name: Display name. Lead with the locality — surveyor's voice.
_schema.site_class: Site classification: municipal_landfill | mswi_bottom_ash_cell | mixed_msw_legacy | leachate_collection_biofilm | construction_demolition_pile.
_schema.location: {country, region, locality} — populated for real sites, partial for archetypal.
_schema.operator_or_excavator: Who pulled the cores or pipes. NEW-MINE consortium for the European ELFM corpus.
_schema.excavation_year: Year the characterization study was performed (not when the cell was filled).
_schema.citations: Primary characterization papers. Use the same short-form 'Author Year Journal Vol:Page' convention as minerals.json.
_schema.evidence_level: Parallel to mineral catalog: landfill_specific (peer-reviewed characterization of this exact site) | program_synthesis (this site contributed to a multi-site review whose findings are reported jointly) | chemistry_archetype (no real-site characterization; the scenario is an archetypal recipe).
_schema.waste_age_years: Approximate age of the waste at characterization time. Drives the dominant_phase default.
_schema.dominant_phase: Leachate phase governing the cell at characterization time: acid (yrs 1-10) | methanogenic (decades) | stable (centuries) | alkaline_cap (cement-dominated) | burn_zone (post-incineration). Multiple lifts in a real cell live in different phases simultaneously; this is the reported governing phase for the characterization horizon.
_schema.substrate_inventory: Array of substrate-token references (matching minerals.json substrate_grows_on tokens) with per-token note describing the documented relics at this site.
_schema.leachate_chemistry: Characteristic ion concentrations for the dominant_phase. Values typical of the phase rather than direct measurement — only Pb/PO4/Cl/Zn/S2 are reported with site-specific pedigree where the citation supports it. Honest about evidence.
_schema.expected_species: Map from mineral catalog ID to {evidence_role, note}. evidence_role: directly_observed (XRD/SEM identification in the cited paper) | implied_by_substrate_chemistry (the relics + leachate make the phase chemically necessary, but the paper does not name it) | predicted_from_program_synthesis (the phase appears in a related ELFM review covering this site without being named in a site-specific paper).
_schema.active_zones: Subset of zone IDs (from data/zones.json) currently dominant or running active chemistry at this scenario's snapshot. The renderer tints only these in the schematic. NOTE: this is a current-chemistry filter, not a crystal-presence filter — crystals nucleated in any historically-active zone persist even after the zone has waned, so the crystal-dot generator places dots based on the mineral's expected zones in zones.json regardless of active_zones state. The two systems are complementary: active_zones = current chemistry territory; crystal dots = accumulated paragenesis since cell closure.
_schema.player_brief: 3-4 sentences in clinical-Borges register — the surveyor introducing the cell. No second-person addresses, no game framing; treat the brief as a corporate-quarterly-report intro paragraph for the working cell.

_audit_summary._notes: v0.1.0 scenario spine. Two NEW-MINE European cells: Mont-Saint-Guibert (BE) at landfill_specific evidence (Garcia Lopez Detritus 8 — direct phase identifications), Halbenrain (AT) at program_synthesis (Hernandez Parrodi Processes 2021 — multi-site review). Together they exercise the schema's two evidence tiers honestly. Future additions slated: an MSWI bottom-ash cell (Saffarzadeh Waste Manage 2011) for the burn-zone chemistry; an LCS biofilm cell (Wilfert/Bennett-Fleming) for the vivianite/struvite scaling assemblage. Each documented landfill_specific mineral in minerals.json will eventually live in at least one scenario; the audit pass that ensures full coverage is deferred until the engine layer can actually consume these specs.

mont_saint_guibert.name: Mont-Saint-Guibert — NEW-MINE characterization cell
citations: Garcia Lopez et al. Detritus 8 (Mont-Saint-Guibert ELFM, fines mineralogy)
Hernandez Parrodi et al. 2021 Processes 9:394 doi:10.3390/pr9020394 (NEW-MINE synthesis)
mont_saint_guibert._active_zones_note: Stable-phase cell at 30 yrs: acidogenic_horizon has been buried by the methanogenic front migrating upward (no longer running active acid chemistry); the other five zones are all currently active. stable_basal is the dominant late-paragenesis zone — the diagnostic Garcia Lopez pyromorphite + sphalerite + Pb-Ca-Cl-PO4 convergence matures here. Anglesite dots will still appear in the (now-untinted) acidogenic_horizon area as relict crystals from the earlier acidic phase.

substrate_inventory.note: Metallic Pb fragments identified by Garcia Lopez et al. as relics of corroded battery plates. Anchors the Pb chemistry.
Fe-Zn alloy fragments identified in the <4.5 mm fines. Substrate for both sphalerite (reducing) and the Zn-sulfate weathering products (oxidizing).
PCB and electrical fittings, implied by the ELFM-program inventory rather than site-specific call-out. Contributes to the diffuse Pb pool.
Decomposed food + paper providing PO4 (food/bone-derived), CO2, and the reducing potential the methanogenic phase has been running on for two decades.
Mixed C&D rubble in the lower lifts; supplies Ca for the late phosphate/chloride convergence.

leachate_chemistry._note: Phase-typical concentrations consistent with the documented phase suite. Garcia Lopez reports phase identification via SEM-EDS; bulk leachate ion measurements are not the focus of that paper. Treat the values as 'consistent with the observed mineralogy' rather than direct field measurements.

pyromorphite.note: Pb-Ca phosphate (pyromorphite-group, Pb5(PO4)3Cl) directly identified in <4.5 mm fines by Garcia Lopez SEM-EDS. The most diagnostic Mont-Saint-Guibert phase — the four-way convergence of Pb (battery), PO4 (organic), Cl (PVC decay), and stable late-phase chemistry was the reason this site became the Pb-chemistry reference cell.

sphalerite.note: ZnS coatings on Fe-Zn alloy substrate, identified in the same fines fraction. Biogenic H2S from sulfate-reducer activity in the methanogenic phase met dissolved Zn from corroding galvanizing.

anglesite.note: Garcia Lopez reports metallic Pb relics and acidic-then-neutralized weathering, but does not name anglesite specifically. Anglesite is the obligate sulfate weathering product of metallic Pb under acidic SO4-bearing leachate; its presence is chemically necessary if the cell ever passed through an acidic phase, which the methanogenic transition implies.

calcite.note: Late-stage Ca-CO3 from the C&D fraction neutralizing residual acid and capping the late paragenesis. Documented broadly in mature MSW cells (Bennett/Fleming clogging review); likely present at Mont-Saint-Guibert by chemistry, not named in the cited fines paper.

mont_saint_guibert.player_brief: Mont-Saint-Guibert cell, Walloon Brabant. Closed 2008, excavated by NEW-MINE in 2018 to characterize the <4.5 mm fines fraction. The cell is the Pb/Zn reference standard for the European ELFM corpus: olive-green pyromorphite hexagonal prisms on lead-acid battery relics, amber sphalerite tetrahedra on Fe-Zn alloy fragments, the four-way Pb-PO4-Cl-Ca convergence reading as deliberately engineered though it accumulated by accident over thirty years of methanogenic stability. Garcia Lopez SEM-EDS catalog is the working ground truth.

halbenrain.name: Halbenrain — NEW-MINE confirmation cell
citations: Hernandez Parrodi et al. 2021 Processes 9:394 doi:10.3390/pr9020394 (NEW-MINE synthesis covering Halbenrain)
halbenrain._active_zones_note: Methanogenic-phase cell at 25 yrs: still has the acidogenic_horizon as an active band (acid chemistry hasn't fully drained into the methanogenic front yet); methanogenic_core is the dominant zone. stable_basal is NOT yet active — late paragenesis hasn't matured to the stable phase. Pyromorphite dots will appear in the (now-untinted) stable_basal area as seed crystals already forming via diffusion from the methanogenic core, but at lower density than Mont-Saint-Guibert's stable-phase yield.

substrate_inventory.note: Battery relics inferred from the NEW-MINE program inventory — typical European municipal cell composition. Halbenrain-specific phase characterization at this granularity is not separately published.
Galvanized fines reported in the program-level review. Chemistry parallels Mont-Saint-Guibert's substrate set.
Food and paper waste supplying PO4 and the reducing potential for a still-active methanogenic phase.
Mixed C&D rubble providing Ca and pH-buffering for late carbonate phases.

leachate_chemistry._note: Methanogenic-phase typical values; Halbenrain-specific bulk leachate measurements are not the focus of the program-synthesis paper. Values picked to be consistent with the documented Mont-Saint-Guibert suite while sitting earlier in the paragenetic sequence (more S2 still active, less stable-phase Pb-Ca-Cl convergence yet).

sphalerite.note: ZnS expected on the same chemical grounds as Mont-Saint-Guibert — galvanized fines + biogenic sulfide. Hernandez Parrodi 2021 covers both cells under one program; site-specific phase reports for Halbenrain at the granularity of Garcia Lopez's Mont-Saint-Guibert SEM-EDS work are not separately published.

pyromorphite.note: Pb-Ca phosphate predicted by the same four-way convergence. The cell is two phase-shifts younger than Mont-Saint-Guibert; the prediction is that pyromorphite is forming but has not yet reached the stable-phase grain sizes Garcia Lopez reported at Mont-Saint-Guibert.

vivianite.note: Fe2+ + PO4 in the active methanogenic phase predicts vivianite scaling on rebar fragments and in the leachate-collection assemblage. Documented in landfill leachate scaling generally (Wilfert 2021 Water Research); chemistry strongly favors it for Halbenrain's phase window.

anglesite.note: Same chemical-necessity argument as Mont-Saint-Guibert — relict acidic-phase weathering of battery Pb under SO4-bearing leachate.

halbenrain.player_brief: Halbenrain cell, Styria. NEW-MINE confirmation excavation in the same program lineage as Mont-Saint-Guibert, characterized at program-synthesis granularity rather than site-specific SEM-EDS. The cell sits two phase-shifts earlier than Mont-Saint-Guibert: still actively methanogenic, sulfide chemistry still running, late-phase pyromorphite seed crystals forming but not yet at the grain sizes the Belgian fines paper reported. Read the cell as a paragenesis-in-progress rather than a closed system.
